front: Explain the mechanism of liposomal drug delivery systems.
back: Liposomes are phospholipid bilayers enclosing aqueous core. PEGylated (stealth) liposomes evade RES, extend circulation. Neutral lipids: DPPC, DSPC. Cholesterol reduces permeability. Active targeting: antibody-ligand conjugation. Doxil (PEGylated doxorubicin) is a clinical example.
